rookie.title: The Final Will
rookie.flavor: Lord Ashworth's funeral was yesterday, yet tonight someone made certain his will would never be read. The inheritance that should have brought joy instead brought death.
rookie.emptyState: The will remains unread. Someone ensured it stays that way.

items.name: Margaret Ashworth
items.detail: Lord Ashworth's devoted sister and closest living relative.

items.name: Thomas Whitmore
items.detail: The family solicitor who handled all legal matters.

items.name: Eleanor Cross
items.detail: Long-serving housekeeper who knew every family secret.

items.name: Poisoned brandy
items.detail: Decanter laced with deadly nightshade from the garden.

items.name: Letter opener
items.detail: Sharp brass blade from Lord Ashworth's writing desk.

items.name: Candlestick
items.detail: Heavy silver piece from the dining room mantle.

items.name: Library
items.detail: Where the victim was found among scattered legal documents.

items.name: Study
items.detail: Lord Ashworth's private office containing his personal safe.

items.name: Drawing room
items.detail: Elegant parlor where the family gathered for meetings.

clues.heading: Garden Shears Found
clues.text: Muddy gardening shears discovered in Eleanor's quarters, with fresh nightshade stems nearby.
clues.alone: Eleanor had recent access to deadly nightshade plants.

links.insight: Eleanor gathered nightshade but Thomas used it to poison the brandy.

clues.heading: Heated Exchange
clues.text: Neighbor reports seeing Margaret and the victim arguing violently through library windows yesterday.
clues.alone: Margaret had a serious conflict with the victim recently.

links.insight: Margaret argued about charity work, not inheritance, proving her innocence.

clues.heading: Debt Records
clues.text: Thomas's personal ledger reveals massive gambling debts totaling three thousand pounds.
clues.alone: Thomas desperately needed money to cover enormous gambling losses.

links.insight: Thomas had financial motive but Eleanor was seen fleeing, creating reasonable doubt.

clues.heading: Brandy Analysis
clues.text: Laboratory confirms the victim's brandy contained lethal nightshade. Thomas's fingerprints on decanter.
clues.alone: Thomas handled the poisoned brandy that killed the victim.

links.insight: Eleanor gathered nightshade but Thomas used it to poison the brandy.

clues.heading: Charity Letters
clues.text: Margaret's correspondence reveals her argument concerned the victim's refusal to donate to orphanages.
clues.alone: Margaret's dispute was about charitable giving, not personal gain.

links.insight: Margaret argued about charity work, not inheritance, proving her innocence.

clues.heading: Fleeing Figure
clues.text: Constable spotted Eleanor leaving the manor grounds hastily near midnight carrying a carpet bag.
clues.alone: Eleanor fled the scene shortly after the estimated time of death.

links.insight: Thomas had financial motive but Eleanor was seen fleeing, creating reasonable doubt.

rookie.epilogue: Thomas Whitmore's gambling addiction drove him to poison Harold Cross with nightshade-laced brandy in the library. His access to legal documents and the victim's trust made the crime possible. Eleanor fled because she discovered the body and panicked, fearing blame. Margaret's charity argument was mere coincidence. Thomas now faces the gallows for his desperate gamble.

clues.heading: Garden Shears Found
clues.text: Muddy gardening shears discovered in Eleanor's quarters, with fresh nightshade stems nearby.
clues.alone: Eleanor had recent access to deadly nightshade plants.

links.insight: Eleanor gathered nightshade but Thomas used it to poison the brandy.

clues.heading: Doctor's Account
clues.text: Dr. Hayes swears he saw Thomas leaving the conservatory at 11:30 PM carrying something.
clues.alone: Thomas was near the plant collection late at night.

links.insight: Dr. Hayes was mistaken about Thomas's identity due to poor lighting.

clues.heading: Debt Records
clues.text: Thomas's personal ledger reveals massive gambling debts totaling three thousand pounds.
clues.alone: Thomas desperately needed money to cover enormous gambling losses.

links.insight: Thomas needed money urgently and had opportunity during the 10-11 PM window.

clues.heading: Brandy Analysis
clues.text: Laboratory confirms the victim's brandy contained lethal nightshade. Thomas's fingerprints on decanter.
clues.alone: Thomas handled the poisoned brandy that killed the victim.

links.insight: Eleanor gathered nightshade but Thomas used it to poison the brandy.

clues.heading: Broken Lamp
clues.text: Shattered oil lamp found in conservatory corridor created very poor lighting conditions.
clues.alone: The conservatory area was nearly dark during the evening.

links.insight: Dr. Hayes was mistaken about Thomas's identity due to poor lighting.

clues.heading: Butler's Timeline
clues.text: Butler confirms Thomas visited the library between 10:00-11:00 PM to retrieve legal documents.
clues.alone: Thomas was present at the crime scene during a crucial hour.

links.insight: Thomas needed money urgently and had opportunity during the 10-11 PM window.

detective.epilogue: Thomas Whitmore poisoned Harold Cross with nightshade-laced brandy in the library between 10:00-11:00 PM. His gambling debts made him desperate for the inheritance he expected to control. Eleanor innocently gathered nightshade for garden pest control. Dr. Hayes mistook someone else for Thomas in the dark conservatory. Thomas's greed cost him everything.

clues.heading: Garden Shears Found
clues.text: Muddy gardening shears discovered in Eleanor's quarters, with fresh nightshade stems nearby.
clues.alone: Eleanor had recent access to deadly nightshade plants.

links.insight: Eleanor gathered nightshade but Thomas used it to poison the brandy.

clues.heading: Doctor's Account
clues.text: Dr. Hayes swears he saw Thomas leaving the conservatory at 11:30 PM carrying something.
clues.alone: Thomas was near the plant collection late at night.

links.insight: Dr. Hayes was mistaken about Thomas's identity due to poor lighting.

clues.heading: Loan Records
clues.text: Bank documents reveal Thomas borrowed against his practice to pay gambling debts last month.
clues.alone: Thomas was under severe financial pressure from gambling losses.

links.insight: Thomas faced ruin from gambling debts and saw Harold Cross's death as financial salvation.

clues.heading: Brandy Analysis
clues.text: Laboratory confirms the victim's brandy contained lethal nightshade. Thomas's fingerprints on decanter.
clues.alone: Thomas handled the poisoned brandy that killed the victim.

links.insight: Eleanor gathered nightshade but Thomas used it to poison the brandy.

clues.heading: Broken Lamp
clues.text: Shattered oil lamp found in conservatory corridor created very poor lighting conditions.
clues.alone: The conservatory area was nearly dark during the evening.

links.insight: Dr. Hayes was mistaken about Thomas's identity due to poor lighting.

clues.heading: Threatening Letter
clues.text: Hidden note threatens Thomas with professional ruin unless gambling debts paid within days.
clues.alone: Thomas faced immediate destruction of his legal career.

links.insight: Thomas faced ruin from gambling debts and saw Harold Cross's death as financial salvation.

inspector.epilogue: Thomas Whitmore poisoned Harold Cross with nightshade-laced brandy in the library between 10:00-11:00 PM, driven by crushing gambling debts. He planned to control the estate's legal affairs and embezzle funds to save his career. Eleanor innocently gathered nightshade for pest control, while Dr. Hayes mistook someone else for Thomas in the darkness. Thomas's desperation transformed him from trusted solicitor to cold-blooded killer.
